text: For online domestic card payments, Stripe charges 2.9% + CA$0.30 per successful card charge.
Stripe does not charge setup fees, monthly fees, or any other hidden fees.
Cards issued outside Canada are charged an additional 0.8% on each successful card charge.
An additional 2% fee applies when currency conversion is required on a charge.
Pre-authorized debit (ACH) payments are charged 1% + CA$0.40 per transaction, capped at CA$5.00 per transaction.
Stripe charges a CA$15.00 dispute fee for each dispute received. If you win the dispute, the fee is returned; if you lose, the fee is not returned.
Instant Payouts cost 1% of the payout volume, with a minimum fee of CA$0.60 per payout.
Stripe is certified to PCI Service Provider Level 1, the most stringent level of certification available in the payments industry.
All card numbers are encrypted at rest with AES-256.
Stripe requires at least TLS 1.2 and automatically blocks requests that use older, less secure versions of TLS.
Stripe produces SOC 1, SOC 2 Type II, and SOC 3 reports annually.
All Stripe internal server-to-server communication is encrypted using mutual TLS (mTLS).
Stripe gives businesses access to 100+ payment methods.
